hero.title: Educational Philosophy | الفلسفة التربوية
hero.subtitle: This page is organized around belief statements, classroom implications, and evidence prompts so the philosophy reads as one coherent narrative.

sections.label: Page Logic
sections.title: How this page should be read
body: Open with a short positioning statement that explains the teaching identity behind the portfolio.
Then move from broad beliefs to concrete practice so every idea is tied to what happens in planning, teaching, and assessment.
points: Keep one main idea in each card.
Use examples from lessons, tools, or student interaction to make each belief credible.
End the page with evidence or reflection rather than abstract statements only.

sections.label: Core Beliefs
sections.title: Recommended belief blocks
sections.description: Each block below can become one stable subsection with its own short evidence paragraph or linked artifact.

cards.label: Belief 01
cards.title: Nature of Learning and the Role of Teaching | طبيعة التعلم ودور التدريس
cards.body: Explain how learning happens best in your context and what the teacher must design, guide, and monitor.

cards.label: Belief 02
cards.title: The Teacher as a Learning Designer | المعلم كمصمم للتعلم
cards.body: Show how lesson structure, sequencing, and resources are intentionally chosen rather than added randomly.

cards.label: Belief 03
cards.title: The Learner's Role in the Learning Process | دور المتعلم في عملية التعلم
cards.body: Describe how learners participate, practice, reflect, and take responsibility inside the learning experience.

cards.label: Belief 04
cards.title: Diversity in Teaching and Addressing Individual Differences | التنوع في التدريس ومعالجة الفروق الفردية
cards.body: Document how instruction is adapted across readiness levels, learning needs, and classroom differences.

cards.label: Belief 05
cards.title: Integrating Technology and Visual Design in Education | دمج التكنولوجيا والتصميم البصري في التعليم
cards.body: Clarify when technology improves understanding, interaction, accessibility, or the quality of educational materials.

cards.label: Belief 06
cards.title: Assessment as a Tool for Improving Learning | التقييم كأداة لتحسين التعلم
cards.body: Frame assessment as feedback and decision-making, not only grading, and connect it to student progress.

cards.label: Belief 07
cards.title: Lifelong Learning | التعلم مدى الحياة
cards.body: Show how your professional growth and reflective habits shape the way you teach and improve.

cards.label: Belief 08
cards.title: Professional Values and Ethics | القيم والأخلاقيات المهنية
cards.body: State the values that guide judgement, fairness, responsibility, and communication in educational settings.

sections.label: Writing Flow
sections.title: Suggested order for final content
sections.description: Use the same sequence for every belief area so the page stays consistent and easy to review.

steps.title: State the belief clearly
steps.body: Start with one focused claim in two or three sentences.

steps.title: Connect the belief to practice
steps.body: Add a lesson move, classroom routine, or instructional design choice that proves the belief.

steps.title: Attach evidence
steps.body: Reference a lesson plan, assessment sample, reflection, or observation note that supports the claim.

hero.title: Other Evidence | أعمال وأنشطة أخرى
hero.subtitle: This section now works as a structured hub. Each subsection represents one evidence stream instead of mixing unrelated artifacts on one page.

sections.label: Section Purpose
sections.title: What belongs here
body: Use this page as an index for supporting evidence that does not fit naturally inside the main teaching-unit sequence.
Each child page should document one theme only, with a clear purpose, supporting artifacts, and a short reflection on impact.
points: Separate workshop evidence from growth records.
Keep peer-visit notes independent from parent communication records.
Use repeated section headings so the reviewer can scan every page quickly.

sections.label: Subsections
sections.title: Organized access points
sections.description: Each subsection below follows the same content logic: context, evidence, reflection, and follow-up.

links.label: Other 01
links.title: Professional Values Scenarios | سيناريوهات القيم المهنية
links.body: Cases that show ethical judgement, professional responsibility, and decision-making in practice.

links.label: Other 02
links.title: Workshop | الورشة
links.body: A dedicated space for workshop context, agenda, proof of participation, and applied outcomes.

links.label: Other 03
links.title: Professional Development Activities Evidence | أدلة أنشطة النمو المهني
links.body: Records of courses, certificates, learning activities, and how they influenced teaching practice.

links.label: Other 04
links.title: Peer Visit Exchange Evidence | أدلة تبادل الزيارات
links.body: Observation notes, feedback exchange, and changes made after professional visits.

links.label: Other 05
links.title: Parent Communication Form | استمارة تواصل مع أولياء الأمور
links.body: Communication context, summary, response, and follow-up actions documented in a clear sequence.

hero.title: Professional Values Scenarios | سيناريوهات القيم المهنية
hero.subtitle: This page is structured to show the scenario, the professional dilemma, the chosen response, and the reasoning behind it.

sections.label: Use Pattern
sections.title: Recommended structure for each scenario
body: Present one scenario at a time so the reviewer can understand the context before reading the analysis.
Focus on judgement, accountability, and the professional values that shaped the final decision.
points: State the situation clearly.
Identify the tension or risk in the case.
Explain the action taken and why it was professionally appropriate.

sections.label: Content Blocks
sections.title: Suggested evidence blocks
sections.description: These blocks keep the scenario page analytical instead of descriptive only.

cards.label: Block 01
cards.title: Scenario Context
cards.body: Who was involved, what happened, and why the case required a professional response.

cards.label: Block 02
cards.title: Values in Tension
cards.body: List the professional values, responsibilities, or institutional expectations that had to be balanced.

cards.label: Block 03
cards.title: Decision and Action
cards.body: Describe the response taken, including communication, documentation, or escalation when needed.

cards.label: Block 04
cards.title: Reflection and Learning
cards.body: Summarize what the case taught you about professional conduct and future decision-making.

hero.title: Workshop | الورشة
hero.subtitle: The workshop page is now arranged around event context, participation evidence, outputs, and classroom transfer.

sections.label: Page Logic
sections.title: What the reviewer should find first
body: Start with the workshop title, date, venue, organizer, and your role in the event.
Then show what was learned, what materials were used, and what changed afterward in practice.
points: Keep logistical details together.
Separate proof of participation from reflective commentary.
Close with outcomes that connect the workshop to teaching practice.

sections.label: Evidence Blocks
sections.title: Recommended workshop sections
sections.description: This layout makes the page feel complete even before the final artifacts are inserted.

cards.label: Block 01
cards.title: Workshop Snapshot
cards.body: Record the workshop name, focus area, facilitator, and participation details.

cards.label: Block 02
cards.title: Agenda and Materials
cards.body: Summarize the session topics and attach slides, handouts, or learning materials where relevant.

cards.label: Block 03
cards.title: Participation Evidence
cards.body: Use certificates, attendance proof, notes, photos, or submitted tasks as direct evidence.

cards.label: Block 04
cards.title: Impact on Practice
cards.body: Explain what changed afterward in lesson design, communication, assessment, or professional habits.

hero.title: Professional Development Activities Evidence | أدلة أنشطة النمو المهني
hero.subtitle: This page is structured as a running record of growth activities, evidence, impact, and next learning targets.

sections.label: Documentation Rule
sections.title: How to keep this page organized
body: Treat each activity as one documented entry with a title, date, provider, outcome, and attached evidence.
Avoid listing certificates only. The strongest version of this page shows how each activity changed practice.
points: Log activities in a consistent order.
Attach proof for each entry.
End each entry with one sentence about classroom or professional impact.

sections.label: Entry Template
sections.title: Recommended content blocks
sections.description: Use the same blocks repeatedly for courses, seminars, online training, and self-directed learning activities.

cards.label: Block 01
cards.title: Activity Record
cards.body: Document the activity title, provider, date, and the main topic addressed.

cards.label: Block 02
cards.title: Evidence Attached
cards.body: Add certificates, screenshots, notes, task submissions, or participation records.

cards.label: Block 03
cards.title: Impact on Practice
cards.body: State what changed in planning, teaching, assessment, or educational technology use after the activity.

cards.label: Block 04
cards.title: Next Development Target
cards.body: Identify the next area to strengthen so growth appears continuous rather than isolated.

hero.title: Peer Visit Exchange Evidence | أدلة تبادل الزيارات
hero.subtitle: This page now separates visit context, observation focus, feedback exchange, and instructional changes made afterward.

sections.label: Visit Pattern
sections.title: How to present each peer visit
body: Document one visit at a time, including who was visited, what was observed, and why the visit mattered professionally.
The strongest evidence here is not the visit itself but the change in practice that followed the feedback exchange.
points: Record the visit purpose and date.
List the observation focus areas.
Show what feedback was received or offered and how it was used.

sections.label: Evidence Blocks
sections.title: Suggested peer-visit sections
sections.description: These blocks keep observation notes, reflection, and action steps in the right order.

cards.label: Block 01
cards.title: Visit Details
cards.body: Identify the class, teacher, date, and purpose of the observation or exchange.

cards.label: Block 02
cards.title: Observation Focus
cards.body: State what you were looking for such as classroom management, assessment routines, questioning, or technology use.

cards.label: Block 03
cards.title: Feedback Exchange
cards.body: Summarize the main feedback points, insights, or professional dialogue from the visit.

cards.label: Block 04
cards.title: Applied Change
cards.body: Explain what was adjusted in your own practice after the visit and how that change was evaluated.

hero.title: Parent Communication Form | استمارة تواصل مع أولياء الأمور
hero.subtitle: The page structure now separates communication context, message summary, response, and follow-up while keeping confidentiality visible.

sections.label: Communication Logic
sections.title: What should be visible on this page
body: Present the reason for communication first, then summarize the message, the parent response, and the agreed next step.
Keep personal details limited and focus on professional communication quality, clarity, and documented follow-up.
points: State the communication purpose clearly.
Summarize the conversation or form content without unnecessary sensitive detail.
Document the outcome and next action.

sections.label: Form Structure
sections.title: Recommended communication blocks
sections.description: These blocks help the form read as a professional record rather than an isolated note.

cards.label: Block 01
cards.title: Communication Context
cards.body: Explain the reason for contact, the student issue or update, and the communication channel used.

cards.label: Block 02
cards.title: Message Summary
cards.body: Capture the core points shared with the parent or guardian in a concise, neutral tone.

cards.label: Block 03
cards.title: Response and Agreement
cards.body: Record the parent response, clarifications, or decisions reached during the interaction.

cards.label: Block 04
cards.title: Follow-up and Professional Notes
cards.body: Add the next step, timeline, and any note about confidentiality, sensitivity, or school procedure.
